from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping; fall back to a generic form.
    verb_map = {
        "almaMater": "studied at",
        "birthDate": "was born on",
        "birthName": "was originally named",
        "birthPlace": "was born in",
        "dateOfRetirement": "retired on",
        "occupation": "worked as",
        "status": "has status",
        "timeInSpace": "spent",
        "mission": "took part in mission",
        "selectedByNasa": "was selected by NASA in",
        "deathDate": "died on",
        "nationality": "was a",
        "servedAsChiefOfTheAstronautOfficeIn": "served as Chief of the Astronaut Office in",
        "title": "held the title",
        "ribbonAward": "received the ribbon",
        "operator": "was operated by",
        "backupPilot": "had backup pilot",
        "commander": "was commanded by",
        "crewMembers": "had crew member",
        "representative": "was represented by",
        "alternativeName": "was also known as",
        "awards": "received",
        "fossil": "has fossil",
        "senators": "has senator",
        "part": "is part of",
        "partsType": "has parts type",
        "higher": "is higher than",
        "isPartOf": "is part of",
        "bird": "has bird",
        "leader": "has leader",
        "affiliation": "is affiliated with",
        "competeIn": "competes in",
        "president": "has president",
        "award": "received award",
        "deathPlace": "died in",
        "gemstone": "has gemstone",
        "mascot": "has mascot",
        "cosparId": "has COSPAR ID",
        "utcOffset": "has UTC offset",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to build richer clauses
    if not triples:
        return ""
    grouped = {}
    for t in triples:
        grouped.setdefault(t.subject, []).append(t)

    def _clean(obj: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes and extra whitespace."""
        obj = obj.strip()
        if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
            obj = obj[1:-1]
        return obj

    sentences = []
    for subject, ts in grouped.items():
        clause_parts = []
        for t in ts:
            verb = verb_map.get(t.predicate, f"has {t.predicate}")
            obj = _clean(t.object)
            if t.predicate == "timeInSpace":
                obj = f"{obj} minutes"
            clause_parts.append(f"{verb} {obj}")

        # Build a clause for the current subject
        if len(clause_parts) == 1:
            clause = f"{subject} {clause_parts[0]}"
        else:
            # Oxford comma style joining
            clause = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1]
        sentences.append(clause)

    # Combine all subject clauses into one fluent sentence
    if len(sentences) == 1:
        final_sentence = sentences[0] + "."
    else:
        final_sentence = ", ".join(sentences[:-1]) + ", and " + sentences[-1] + "."

    # Capitalize first character
    return final_sentence[0].upper() + final_sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
